file_name: VirtuosoSurgicalInc_20191227_1-A_EX1A-6 MAT CTRCT_11933379_EX1A-6 MAT CTRCT_License Agreement.txt

perturbation.original_text: 2.5 "NET SALES" shall mean gross sales revenues and fees billed by Company from the sale of LICENSED PRODUCT(S) less trade discounts allowed, refunds, returns and recalls, and sales taxes. In the event that Company sells a LICENSED PRODUCT(S) in combination with other ingredients or substances or as part of a kit, the NET SALES for purposes of royalty payments shall be based on the sales revenues and fees received from the entire combination or kit.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.5 "NET SALES" shall mean gross sales revenues and fees billed by Company from the sale of LICENSED PRODUCT(S) less trade discounts allowed, refunds, returns and recalls, and sales taxes. However, for internal accounting purposes only, NET SALES may also be calculated using a different methodology as defined by the Company's CFO, in accordance with generally accepted accounting practices. In the event that Company sells a LICENSED PRODUCT(S) in combination with other ingredients or substances or as part of a kit, the NET SALES for purposes of royalty payments shall be based on the sales revenues and fees received from the entire combination or kit.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of "NET SALES" provides a clear and specific calculation method. The added language introduces ambiguity by allowing an alternative calculation method determined by the Company's CFO, potentially conflicting with the original definition for royalty payments. This creates an in-text contradiction because the NET SALES that is used for royalty payments may be using 2 definitions that are contradicting, making it difficult to calculate.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.3 Running Royalties: Company shall pay to JHU, a running royalty as set forth in Exhibit A, for each LICENSED PRODUCT(S) sold and each LICENSED SERVICE(S) provided, based upon NET SALES and NET SERVICE REVENUES respectively, for the term of this Agreement. Such payments shall be made within thirty (30) days of the end of each calendar quarter following first commercial sale of LICENSED PRODUCT or providing LICENSED SERVICES. All non-US taxes related to LICENSED PRODUCT(S) and LICENSED SERVICE(S) sold under this Agreement shall be paid by Company and shall not be deducted from royalty or other payments due to JHU.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an alternative definition for "NET SALES" that can be determined by the company's CFO for internal accounting purposes. This contradicts the original definition used for royalty payments to JHU as outlined in Section 4.3. Section 4.3 explicitly states that royalty payments are based upon NET SALES, implying a single, consistent definition. Allowing a different calculation method introduces ambiguity and the potential for a lower royalty payment if the CFO's calculation results in a smaller NET SALES figure, thus creating a direct conflict and in-text contradiction according to Misaligned Terminology, as the royalty payment calculation now has two potentially conflicting definitions of NET SALES.

perturbation.original_text: 3.1 License Granted: Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement and to non-exclusive license agreements executed prior to the EFFECTIVE DATE, JHU hereby grants to the Company a non-exclusive, non-transferable license to make, have made, import, offer for sale and sell the LICENSED PRODUCT(S) and the LICENSED SERVICE(S) in the United States and worldwide under the PATENT RIGHT(S) in the LICENSED FIELD.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.1 License Granted: Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement and to non-exclusive license agreements executed prior to the EFFECTIVE DATE, JHU hereby grants to the Company a non-exclusive, non-transferable license to make, have made, import, offer for sale and sell the LICENSED PRODUCT(S) and the LICENSED SERVICE(S) in the United States and worldwide under the PATENT RIGHT(S) in the LICENSED FIELD, contingent upon the Company's adherence to ethical business practices as determined solely by JHU.
perturbation.explanation: By adding "contingent upon the Company's adherence to ethical business practices as determined solely by JHU", the term "non-exclusive license" is directly contradicted because, implicitly it is making the license revocable under a subjective condition (ethics) assessed unilaterally by JHU, making the license to be interpreted as an exclusive license with the possibility of being revoked by JHU.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.1 License Granted: Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement and to non-exclusive license agreements executed prior to the EFFECTIVE DATE, JHU hereby grants to the Company a non-exclusive, non-transferable license to make, have made, import, offer for sale and sell the LICENSED PRODUCT(S) and the LICENSED SERVICE(S) in the United States and worldwide under the PATENT RIGHT(S) in the LICENSED FIELD, contingent upon the Company's adherence to ethical business practices as determined solely by JHU.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contingency ('contingent upon the Company's adherence to ethical business practices as determined solely by JHU') to the grant of the non-exclusive license. This contradicts the nature of a non-exclusive license, which should be freely exercisable within its scope, by giving JHU the power to effectively revoke or suspend the license based on a subjective criterion. The original text, defining the license as non-exclusive, does not include this contingency, thereby establishing a clear contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 6.3 Termination by JHU: JHU, at its option, may terminate this Agreement and the license granted herein if Company has not made any sales of LICENSED PRODUCT(S) or LICENSED SERVICE(S) in any period of four consecutive quarters, after the initial commercial sale of the PRODUCT(S) or LICENSED SERVICE(S).
perturbation.changed_text: 6.3 Termination by JHU: JHU, at its option, may terminate this Agreement and the license granted herein if Company has not made any sales of LICENSED PRODUCT(S) or LICENSED SERVICE(S) in any period of four consecutive quarters, after the initial commercial sale of the PRODUCT(S) or LICENSED SERVICE(S). However, JHU is only able to terminate if 6.3. is used within the first year of the product being sold. After the 1st year JHU is no longer able to terminate the agreement with what is being stated in 6.3.
perturbation.explanation: The added sentence restricts JHU's termination right described previously in the text to a 1 year period. This restricts JHU from being able to terminate this agreement after a 1 year period for reasons of not selling, contradictory with what has been previously stated in the original text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.3 Termination by JHU: JHU, at its option, may terminate this Agreement and the license granted herein if Company has not made any sales of LICENSED PRODUCT(S) or LICENSED SERVICE(S) in any period of four consecutive quarters, after the initial commercial sale of the PRODUCT(S) or LICENSED SERVICE(S). However, JHU is only able to terminate if 6.3. is used within the first year of the product being sold. After the 1st year JHU is no longer able to terminate the agreement with what is being stated in 6.3.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a limitation on JHU's termination rights under section 6.3, restricting the termination option to the first year after the product's commercial sale. The original text does not have this limitation. This directly contradicts the broader termination right initially granted to JHU, as it creates a temporal constraint that did not previously exist, leading to an inconsistency in the agreement regarding when JHU can exercise its termination rights based on sales performance. Therefore, the changed text creates a strong in-text contradiction according to the instructions.
